name: Ghost Palace Hotel
description: An abandoned hotel in the highlands of Bali is shrouded in stories of ghosts, curses, and corruption.

name: Tanah Lot
description: Isolated atop this Balinese sea crag is a lovely Hindu temple that is said to be guarded by sea snakes.

name: Pura Lempuyang Luhur
description: Bali's remote "gateway to heaven" is one of the most sacred places on the island.

name: Sacred Monkey Forest (Mandala Wisata Wenara Wana)
description: Hindu temple of the dead home to hundreds of long-tailed macaques.

name: Taman Festival
description: A haunting theme park is slowly being swallowed by the jungle.

name: Goa Gajah
description: The name of this ancient Indonesian temple translates as "Elephant Cave" but it could be more accurately called "The Hellmouth."

name: Gereja Ayam
description: This massive chapel in the middle of the Indonesian forest is both haunting and fowl.

name: Komodo National Park
description: A sprawling nature preserve harbors the world's only living dragons.

name: Trunyan
description: One native village in Bali lays their dead at the foot of a holy tree whose natural fragrance seems to hide the stench as the bodies decompose.

name: Padar Island
description: Once home to Komodo dragons, this island dreamscape has a rare combination of white, pink, and black sand beaches.

name: Pura Ulun Danu Bratan
description: This tiny lake temple dedicated to a water goddess seems to float on the surface.

name: Ogoh-Ogoh Museum
description: A small museum full of giant effigies of Bali's demons and monsters.

name: Luhur Uluwatu Temple
description: This Balinese sea temple is thought to be guarded by the hundreds of monkeys that live around it.

name: Millennium Bridge
description: One of the largest bamboo bridges in Asia, located right next to a bamboo school.

name: Kelimutu Volcano
description: Three wildly colorful calderas top this otherworldly volcano chain.

name: Bali's (Other) Abandoned Plane
description: The wing of this deserted Boeing 737 almost touches the Dunkin' Donuts next door.

name: Jembatan Akar
description: This Sumatran root bridge took almost 30 years to knit itself together.

name: Tirta Gangga Water Palace
description: Sacred waters rescued from the wrath of a volcano.

name: Kampung Warna-Warni Jodipan (Village of Color)
description: This Indonesian village was revitalized by a vibrant rainbow paint job.

name: Borobudur
description: Hundreds of Buddhas in a re-discovered Indonesian temple.

name: Prambanan
description: This massive complex is made up of hundreds of smaller temples that date back to the ninth century.

name: Symon's Art Zoo
description: Exotic, erotic, and esoteric, one artist's live-in Bali gallery brings a splash of color and madness to its surroundings.

name: Kawah Ijen
description: Think you have a tough job? Try carrying 200 kilos through a cloud of sulfur down the side of a volcano.

name: Prehistoric Cave Art of Maros Pangkep
description: These 40,000-year-old stenciled hands are older than the famous cave art in France and Spain.

name: Liang Bua Cave
description: A real-life "hobbit" cave in Flores, Indonesia.

name: Kampung Tridi
description: Bright Indonesian villages painted in a rainbow of competing color schemes.

name: Pura Goa Lawah
description: This sacred cave temple is home to thousands of bats and according to legend, a giant snake king.

name: Amed Beach
description: The inky volcanic sand leads to reefs teeming with aquatic life waiting to be discovered by snorkelers and divers.

name: Garuda Wisnu Kencana Statue
description: It took 28 years to build this massive statue of the Hindu god Vishnu riding a mythical bird.

name: Rabbit Tree Hostel
description: A crazy maze of a hostel, where every trap door and secret entrance brings you to a new wacky, wonderful room.

name: Gunung Padang
description: These broken shards of ancient ruin may hide an ancient pyramid.

name: The Funeral Rites of Torajaland
description: Where cemeteries are carved into the side of cliffs and infants are buried in the hollows of trees.

name: Lengkuas Island
description: A hundred and twenty year old twelve story lighthouse located on a beautiful Indonesian island.

name: 'Nest'
description: This underwater sculpture park is the basis for a manmade coral reef.

name: The Sidoarjo Mud Volcano
description: A mud volcano in Indonesia spews out 50,000 cubic meters of mud every day.

name: Solo Batik Carnival
description: Annual street carnival celebrating Indonesian heritage.

name: Abandoned Bounty Beach Bungalows Resort
description: A Balinese resort, empty and trapped in time.

name: Taman Sari Underground Mosque
description: Subterranean place of worship in Indonesia.

name: Londa Burial Caves
description: The unique funeral practices of the Toraja can be seen here with graves carved into caves and hung off cliffs.

name: The Toba Caldera
description: Site of the largest volcanic eruption in the last 25 million years, one which may have nearly wiped out humanity altogether.

name: Surosowan Palace
description: A palace-fortress ruin in Indonesia complete with legends of two magical tigers, eight bathing angels, and a wish-granting cannon.

name: Amorphophallus titanum
description: A monster in the plant world, up to 10 feet tall and smelling of death.

name: Blanco Renaissance Museum
description: Visitors to this museum will find architectural oddities, an art gallery, and an unlikely bird sanctuary.

name: Candi Sukuh
description: This Javanese-Hindu temple from the 15th century is unmissable for anyone with even a passing interest in erotic art.

name: Wehea Forest
description: Explore a uniquely pristine Borneo forest, now protected by former headhunters turned tour guides.

name: Spider Web Rice Fields
description: The unique design was created by the traditional way communal rice paddies were divided among the indigenous people.

name: Pura Tirta Empul
description: One of the holiest places in the region, devotees come to Tirta Empul to bathe in the holy spring water.

name: An Nurumi Mosque
description: An Indonesian mosque styled after St. Basil's Cathedral.

name: Neptune Bar
description: A mostly desolate bar on a remote island hosts hundreds of sailors for a few days each year.

name: Anak Krakatau
description: The second generation of a mighty volcano rises from the sea.

name: Bayung Gede Placenta Graveyard
description: In this Balinese village, coconut shells containing placentas are hung from trees.

name: Mie Lethek Garuda
description: In this rare traditional factory, three generations of know-how, several strong cows, and a one-ton mortar come together to make Yogyakarta's famous "ugly noodles."

name: Grasberg Mine
description: This mile-wide crater high in the mountains of Indonesia is the largest gold mine in the world.

name: Ternate Island
description: The remote island home of the overlooked explorer who co-discovered the theory of evolution by natural selection.

name: Sipiso-piso Waterfall
description: One of Indonesia's tallest, this waterfall takes a 390-foot plunge into the Lake Toba caldera.

name: Gunung Bromo
description: Of five volcanoes located in the massive Tengger caldera, this cone is the youngest and most active.

name: The Equator Monument
description: This monument marks where the exact middle of the world used to be.

name: Mount Tambora
description: Mount Tambora is the site of the largest and deadliest volcanic eruption in recorded human history.

name: Tsunami 'Noah's Ark'
description: After a 2004 tsunami, this stranded boat was left in place.

name: Pemuteran Biorock
description: A unique project to help restore coral reefs in Bali.

name: Barelang Bridge
description: A striking series of six bridges connect islands in Indonesia.

name: Goal Bola-Bola
description: Concrete balls on wires constructed to stop train surfing in Indonesia.

name: Nias Ceremonial Stone Jump
description: Warrior training: The Indonesian tourist trap.

name: Pan Am Flight 812 Memorial
description: A lonely memorial to the 107 victims of Pan Am Flight 812, which crashed in Bali in 1974.

name: Besakih Temple
description: Bali’s Mother Temple, a breathtaking complex that rises serenely up the side of an active volcano.

name: Fort Oranje
description: This 17th-century fort, built by the Dutch to control a piece of the international spice trade, is now a city park.

name: Mount Argopuro
description: The longest hiking route on the island of Java leads to this inactive volcano.

name: Indonesia’s first escalator
description: The country’s first escalator is located in department store filled with batiks and beautiful Indonesian products.

name: Sunyaragi Cave Park
description: This historic 17th-century site once served as a secluded meditation retreat.

name: Yesus Kase Berkat
description: A massive statue depicts Jesus as if he's about to take off in flight.

name: Gunung Kemukus
description: This hilltop shrine is colloquially known as "Sex Mountain" for the ritual practices once conducted there.

name: Surabaya Submarine Monument
description: A WWII-era Soviet submarine offers visitors a glimpse into Indonesia's naval history.

name: Yogyakarta Station Steam Locomotive Monument
description: A preserved ancient Marshall Britannia steam locomotive on display at a train station.

name: Bali's Abandoned Plane
description: Someone left a Boeing 737 in a field in southern Bali.
